metadata.title: Your Version — AI+1 series 3/4
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: The quote-not-subscription model is what makes me want to try this, since no platform fee completely changes how I'd budget. Help me: (1) list what a fair custom quote should cover given I get videos, exam banks, and transform rights; (2) sketch how I'd rebrand and reorder the material inside a CLI; (3) pin down where 'our content, your judgment' draws the line, so I know precisely what I'm paying for versus what always remains mine.

props.command: The quote-not-subscription model is what makes me want to try this, since no platform fee completely changes how I'd budget. Help me: (1) list what a fair custom quote should cover given I get videos, exam banks, and transform rights; (2) sketch how I'd rebrand and reorder the material inside a CLI; (3) pin down where 'our content, your judgment' draws the line, so I know precisely what I'm paying for versus what always remains mine.

beats.narration_text: Step one: email info at humanitarians dot A I. Tell us the course, your audience, and what custom means for you. We quote a price for your version. No subscription, no platform fee — a quote.

props.heading: One email, one quote.
props.body:
Email info@humanitarians.ai.
Name the course + your audience + what custom means.

We quote a price. No subscription. No platform fee.

beats.narration_text: What ships: the base videos, the exam banks, and — the part that matters — the rights to transform them. Into your book. Your course. Your workshop. Your brand on it.

props.heading: Transform rights included.
props.body:
The zip ships:
— base videos
— exam banks
— rights to transform them

Your book. Your course. Your workshop. Your brand.

beats.narration_text: It opens in any C L I — Claude Code, Codex, next year's tool. Rewrite the examples for your industry, cut what you do not teach, add what you do. Our content, your judgment.

props.heading: Any CLI. Your judgment.
props.body:
Opens in Claude Code, Codex, or whichever CLI you use.
Rewrite examples. Cut what you don't teach. Add what you do.

Our content, your judgment.

beats.narration_text: Before the reveal, one question: what is the one thing we do not sell you? Think about what stays out of the zip — and what was never ours in the first place.

props.command: what is the one thing not in the zip?

beats.narration_text: The judgment. We sell the starting point — vetted videos, tested exams — but what to teach, what to cut, what your learners actually need: that was never ours to sell. The zip makes you faster. It does not make you optional.

props.heading: You stay the teacher.
props.body:
We sell the starting point — vetted videos, tested exams.

What to teach, what to cut, what learners need:
that was never ours to sell.

The zip makes you faster. Not optional.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. One page. Email, quote, zip. Videos, exams, and the rights to make them yours — in whatever C L I you already use.

artifactLines: Email info@humanitarians.ai: the course + your audience → a quoted price. No subscription.
The zip: base videos · exam banks · transform rights — your book, your course, your workshop.
Opens in any CLI — rewrite, rebrand, reorder. No platform, no lock-in.
Phase gate: we ship the base · you transform it · your learners get your version.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this into Claude and send what comes out. The quote costs nothing — and the zip is the fastest course you will never have to build from scratch.

props.command: Draft the email to info@humanitarians.ai: name the course I want, describe my audience in two sentences, and ask what a custom version costs and exactly what ships in the zip.
